metadata.title: Mobile-platform security review sub-rubric
metadata.description: Mobile-specific security criteria layered atop the universal base security rubric. Covers certificate pinning, secure storage (Keychain/Keystore), root/jailbreak detection, code obfuscation, local data encryption, IPC security (deep links, intents), and WebView hardening.

severity_rules.pattern: App talks to its own backend over TLS without pinning the server's public-key/cert; any compromised CA in the device trust store can MITM the connection.
severity_rules.description: Certificate pinning blocks MITM via compromised or malicious intermediate CAs and is a baseline expectation for apps handling credentials, financial data, or PII.
severity_rules.remediation: Pin SPKI hashes for at least the leaf and one backup intermediate; rotate via app updates; use App Transport Security pinning (iOS) or Network Security Config (Android).

severity_rules.pattern: Certificate pinning is implemented but bypasses include `setHostnameVerifier(ALLOW_ALL)`, `NSAllowsArbitraryLoads`, or a debug-only TrustManager left in the release build.
severity_rules.description: A bypass left in production code defeats the entire pinning posture; common cause of OWASP MASVS failures.
severity_rules.remediation: Strip debug-only network code from release builds via build flavours; CI-check for ATS exceptions and AllowAllHostnameVerifier in shipped binaries.

severity_rules.pattern: Sensitive data (auth tokens, refresh tokens, user PII) stored in UserDefaults / SharedPreferences / NSDocumentsDirectory in plaintext.
severity_rules.description: UserDefaults and SharedPreferences are unencrypted by default and are extracted trivially on jailbroken / rooted devices and from un-encrypted device backups.
severity_rules.remediation: Use Keychain Services (iOS) with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly, or Android Keystore + EncryptedSharedPreferences for tokens.

severity_rules.pattern: Cryptographic keys are hard-coded in source, embedded in the binary as strings, or derived from device-stable identifiers (IMEI, UDID).
severity_rules.description: Hard-coded keys are recovered with a 5-minute strings(1) sweep and immediately published. Device-stable identifiers do not provide secret entropy.
severity_rules.remediation: Generate keys in Secure Enclave / Android Keystore; never persist private keys to user-readable storage; rotate via server-issued tokens.

severity_rules.pattern: App handling regulated data (banking, health, enterprise) ships without any root/jailbreak detection or with detection logic that any commodity bypass tool defeats.
severity_rules.description: Root/jailbreak gives attackers full control over the app sandbox and runtime, enabling token theft, traffic inspection, and tampering. Root-detection is part of the MASVS-RESILIENCE controls.
severity_rules.remediation: Combine multiple signals (su binary, Magisk markers, mount-mode checks); fail closed on detection for high-stakes flows; harden the detection code itself against patching.

severity_rules.pattern: Release Android build ships without R8 / ProGuard obfuscation; iOS release build ships with debug symbols and unstripped Swift symbol metadata.
severity_rules.description: Symbol-rich binaries make reverse engineering trivial — class names, methods, and even proprietary algorithms are recovered with a hex editor.
severity_rules.remediation: Enable R8 with aggressive obfuscation rules on Android release variants; strip Swift reflection metadata and dSYM from the shipped IPA; upload symbols separately to the crash service.

severity_rules.pattern: App stores user-content data (notes, photos, conversation history) on disk without encryption-at-rest; relies solely on file-system protection that is bypassed on jailbroken devices and stale backups.
severity_rules.description: Plaintext local data is extracted from un-encrypted device backups and from compromised devices. Encryption-at-rest is required by HIPAA / PCI-DSS / GDPR for regulated data.
severity_rules.remediation: Use FileProtectionType.complete (iOS) or EncryptedFile (Android Jetpack Security) with keys held in the platform keystore.

severity_rules.pattern: Deep-link / universal-link handler trusts URL parameters without validation; or Android Intent receiver lacks `exported=false` / `android:permission` and accepts data from any caller.
severity_rules.description: Unvalidated deep-link handlers are a top mobile attack surface — a malicious app or webpage triggers protected actions in the victim app (intent redirection, account takeover).
severity_rules.remediation: Validate every URL parameter against an allow-list; require android:exported=false for internal IntentFilters; verify Universal-Link associations server-side.

severity_rules.pattern: WebView embedded in the app enables JavaScript and addJavascriptInterface (Android) / WKUserContentController without restricting bridge surface; loads remote URLs from un-trusted origins.
severity_rules.description: Bridged JavaScript that exposes native methods is a remote-code-execution surface; loading attacker-controlled HTML in a privileged WebView is a known mobile vulnerability class.
severity_rules.remediation: Disable JavaScript unless required; never expose addJavascriptInterface to remote content; restrict allowed origins; use WKWebView's process-isolated content world.
